title: 10 best herbal creams for plantar fasciitis

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with plantar fasciitis because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The cream's active compounds, such as camphor and menthol, penetrate deep into the skin to reduce swelling and ease pain in the affected area. Additionally, sage's natural antioxidants help to soothe and calm irritated tissues, promoting healing and reducing inflammation in the plantar fascia. This combination of actions helps to alleviate symptoms of plantar fasciitis, providing relief from discomfort and stiffness in the heel and ankle region.
remedy_properties: Ant-inflammatory compounds: Sage creams contain compounds like rosmarinic acid, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain associated with plantar fasciitis.

Pain-relieving flavonoids: The flavonoids present in sage creams, such as apigenin and luteolin, have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate pain and discomfort caused by plantar fasciitis.

Antioxidant properties: Sage creams contain antioxidants like vitamins A and E, which help protect the skin and underlying tissues from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy tissue repair and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with plantar fasciitis.
Barks: The bark of the sage plant contains tannins, which can help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort caused by plantar fasciitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry fresh salvia officinalis leaves in a warm air circulation drying system for 2 weeks.
Combine 30g of dried salvia officinalis leaves with 100ml of olive oil in a glass jar and store for 4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into another container discarding the solids after 4 hours.
Mix 50ml of the infused oil with 20g of beeswax and heat gently over low heat stirring constantly until fully melted in 15 minutes.
Pour the melted mixture into small glass jars and allow to cool and solidify at room temperature within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood creams helps with plantar fasciitis because it contains thujone, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. When applied topically to the affected area, wormwood cream reduces swelling and pain in the heel and arch of the foot, providing relief from the discomfort associated with plantar fasciitis. Additionally, wormwood's antispasmodic effects help relax the muscles and tendons in the foot, reducing strain on the plantar fascia and promoting healing. This natural remedy can be a valuable addition to traditional treatments for plantar fasciitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Wormwood contains bioactive compounds such as camazolon, which reduce inflammation and swelling in the affected area, providing relief from pain and discomfort associated with plantar fasciitis.

Antioxidant and anti-irritant properties: The antioxidants and anti-irritants present in wormwood help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation caused by repetitive strain or overuse injuries like plantar fasciitis.

Analgesic and pain-relieving compounds: Wormwood contains analgesics such as thujone, which work together with other bioactive compounds to provide natural relief from chronic pain and discomfort in the heel and ankle areas.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of wormwood contain bioactive compounds like sesquiterpenoids and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate pain and inflammation associated with plantar fasciitis.
Barks: The barks of wormwood are rich in tannins and phenolic acids, which have astringent and anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried artemisia absinthium leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered artemisia absinthium with 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler, heating the mixture for 10 minutes at 60°c.
Add 20g of beeswax to the mixture and heat it for an additional 5 minutes until fully incorporated and melted.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool for 30 minutes before whipping it with a hand mixer for 2 minutes until light and fluffy.
Transfer the cream to a sterilized container, seal it tightly, and store it in the refrigerator for up to 6 months at a temperature of 2°c to 8°c.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel creams helps with plantar fasciitis because of their unique combination of anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The astringent compounds in witch hazel, such as tannins, help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the affected area. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing effects help to soften and hydrate the skin, making it easier for the plantar fascia to move smoothly and reducing stiffness. As a result, using an herbal American witch hazel cream can provide fast relief from plantar fasciitis symptoms, allowing individuals to resume their normal activities with comfort and confidence.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Witch Hazel cream contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain associated with Plantar Fasciitis, allowing for improved mobility and reduced discomfort.
Antibacterial: Witch Hazel's antibacterial properties can help prevent infection in the affected area, promoting healing and reducing the risk of complications, such as abscesses or cellulitis.

Astringent: Witch Hazel's astringent properties can help to reduce swelling and ease pain by constricting blood vessels and reducing fluid buildup in the affected area, providing relief from Plantar Fasciitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, roots are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help alleviate pain and swelling associated with plantar fasciitis.
Barks: The barks of the American Witch Hazel contain salicin, a natural analgesic similar to aspirin, which helps reduce inflammation and relieve discomfort caused by plantar fasciitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried witch hazel bark from hamamelis virginiana plants weighing approximately one ounce.
Combine the harvested witch hazel bark with three-quarters cup of olive oil and two tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 30 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 180°f.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to room temperature before adding one teaspoon of vitamin e oil and one teaspoon of aloe vera gel.
Stir the mixture well and pour it into sterilized glass jars for storage and use as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with plantar fasciitis because they provide a soothing and comforting relief to the affected area. The herbal compounds present in licorice cream, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain caused by plantar fasciitis. Additionally, the moisturizing properties of licorice cream can help hydrate the skin and tissues around the heel, reducing stiffness and discomfort. As a result, herbal licorice creams can provide effective relief for individuals suffering from plantar fasciitis.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce swelling and pain associated with plantar fasciitis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Licoricidin: This component has analgesic and anti-pyretic effects, which help to alleviate pain and discomfort in the heel and ankle region caused by plantar fasciitis.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in herbal licorice creams have antioxidant properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the affected area, promoting healing and reducing scar tissue formation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice roots contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing pain and swelling associated with plantar fasciitis.

Barks: Licorice barks have been traditionally used to treat various skin conditions, including inflammation and irritation, which can be beneficial for soothing and calming plantar fasciitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Sterilize glass containers and utensils using hot water and soap for 20 minutes.
Combine 200g of petroleum jelly with 50g of beeswax in a double boiler and melt.
Add 10g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder and stir until well combined and dissolved after 5 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool to 40°c before whipping for 10 minutes to incorporate air.
Pour the mixture into sterilized containers and allow it to set at room temperature for 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with plantar fasciitis because they contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe and calm irritated tissues. The creams also help to reduce pain and discomfort by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are chemicals that cause pain and inflammation. Additionally, pot marigold contains natural oils that moisturize and soften the skin, reducing dryness and cracking that can exacerbate plantar fasciitis symptoms. Overall, using herbal pot marigold creams as part of a comprehensive treatment plan can provide effective relief for plantar fasciitis sufferers.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Pot marigold contains flavonoids and terpenoids that have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with plantar fasciitis.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in pot marigold cream can help protect the damaged tissues in the foot from further oxidative stress, promoting healing and reducing the risk of chronic inflammation.
Wound-healing compounds: Pot marigold contains saponins, which have been shown to enhance wound healing by increasing collagen synthesis, improving tissue elasticity, and reducing scar formation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids, roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling associated with plantar fasciitis.
Barks: Containing astringent compounds, barks help to constrict blood vessels, reducing inflammation and alleviating discomfort in the heel and sole.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers at dawn for optimal potency and quality.
Dry the harvested calendula flowers in a warm, dark place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Blend 100g of dried calendula flowers with 200ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking daily to allow for optimal extraction of active compounds.
Strain and filter the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with plantar fasciitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the affected area. The cream's gentle penetration into the skin reduces swelling and pain in the heel and arch regions, allowing for easier movement and reduced stiffness. Additionally, chamomile's natural antispasmodic effects help relax the muscles and tissues surrounding the plantar fascia, reducing tension and strain that can exacerbate the condition. As a result, herbal chamomile creams provide effective relief from plantar fasciitis symptoms, promoting a faster recovery and improved overall foot health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area, making it an effective natural remedy for plantar fasciitis.
Antioxidant: Chamomile's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and damage to tissues, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and pain associated with plantar fasciitis.
Calming and relaxing: Chamomile has a calming effect on the skin and muscles, helping to relax tense foot muscles and reduce discomfort and pain associated with plantar fasciitis.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, chamomile flowers help reduce pain and swelling associated with plantar fasciitis.
Roots: Containing natural analgesics, chamomile roots provide soothing relief from foot pain and stiffness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder within an hour.
Combine the powdered chamomile with 20 grams of beeswax pellets, 40 grams of coconut oil, and 40 grams of shea butter in a double boiler setup on medium heat for 30 minutes.
Stir the mixture every 10 minutes until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 160°f to 180°f after 20 minutes.
Remove the double boiler from heat and let it cool slightly before adding 5 drops of tea tree essential oil for its antibacterial properties and mixing well for 1 minute.
Pour the cream mixture into small glass jars, label them with dates and ingredients, and store them in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or freeze for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with plantar fasciitis because it targets the underlying inflammation that causes pain and discomfort in the heels and soles of the feet. The anti-inflammatory properties of stinging nettle extract reduce swelling and alleviate pressure on the plantar fascia, allowing for improved mobility and reduced stiffness. Additionally, its soothing effects calm irritation and promote relaxation, providing instant relief from the chronic pain associated with plantar fasciitis. Regular application can lead to long-term reduction in symptoms and improved overall foot health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which help reduce inflammation in the plantar fascia and surrounding tissues.

Pain-relieving properties: Nettle creams contain analgesic and anti-nociceptive compounds that help reduce pain and discomfort associated with plantar fasciitis, allowing for improved mobility and reduced stiffness.

Antioxidant activity: Antioxidants in stinging nettle creams help protect the plantar fascia and surrounding tissues from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy tissue repair and reducing scar tissue formation.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried urtica dioica leaves in amounts of 20 grams for every 100 grams of cream base.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered urtica dioica with 200 milliliters of olive oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches 65°c to 70°c.
Pour the warm mixture into sterilized containers, allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with plantar fasciitis because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. The cream's topical application allows for direct delivery of these active ingredients to the site of discomfort, providing quick relief from stiffness and soreness. Additionally, dandelion's natural antioxidants help promote tissue repair and regeneration, allowing for improved flexibility and reduced risk of future flare-ups.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion contains anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol and sesquiterpenes, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with plantar fasciitis.

Seeds: Dandelion seeds contain lignans, a type of polyphenol that has been shown to have analgesic and antispasmodic effects, making them effective in soothing plantar fasciitis pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried taraxacum officinale root bark and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered root bark with 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat, stirring constantly for 30 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated, increasing heat to medium-low and stirring for an additional 5 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a consistency suitable for whipping, which should take approximately 20 minutes.
Whip the cooled mixture in a stand mixer with a whisk attachment for 2-3 minutes until it forms a smooth, creamy consistency, then pour into glass jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St john's wort creams helps with plantar fasciitis because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as hyperforin and hypericin, work to reduce swelling and ease pain in the affected area, providing quick relief from discomfort. By penetrating deep into the skin, St john's wort creams help to relax tense muscles and tendons, reducing tension on the plantar fascia and promoting healing. This natural remedy can be applied directly to the heel and ankle area, making it a convenient and effective treatment option for plantar fasciitis sufferers.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's active compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, can reduce inflammation in the affected area, alleviating pain and swelling associated with Plantar Fasciitis.

Antioxidant properties: St John's Wort's antioxidant activity helps protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and tissue degeneration in plantar fascia.

Pain-relieving properties: The cream contains analgesic compounds that may help block pain signals to the brain, providing relief for patients with Plantar Fasciitis who experience sharp or burning pain in their heel and foot.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St John's Wort are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and swelling associated with plantar fasciitis.

Leaves: The leaves contain hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them an effective ingredient for topical creams to alleviate plantar fasciitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest st john's wort flowers at peak bloom and dry them thoroughly in a well-ventilated area for 2 weeks.
Combine dried st john's wort flowers with coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler at a temperature of 160°f for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, discarding solids, and allow it to cool and solidify over 60 minutes.
Add vitamin e oil and peppermint essential oil to the cooled cream in amounts of 2 teaspoons and 10 drops respectively.
Package the final cream in sterilized tubes or jars, labeling them with date, ingredients, and instructions for use as a topical application 3 times daily for 6 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with plantar fasciitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. The cream's natural cooling sensation also provides relief from discomfort and stiffness, allowing for improved mobility and reduced strain on the foot. Additionally, fennel's antispasmodic properties help relax tense muscles and tendons, further alleviating symptoms of plantar fasciitis. remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare leaves in abundance ensuring freshness for optimal potency and flavor.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 60 degrees celsius for six hours.
Combine one part dried foeniculum vulgare leaves with three parts coconut oil in a double boiler.
Steep the mixture in boiling water for 30 minutes allowing the active compounds to infuse and extract.
Filter the resulting liquid using cheesecloth and transfer it to a sterilized glass container for storage.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried melissa officinalis leaves from reputable sources and grind into fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine 20g of ground melissa officinalis with 100g of sweet almond oil in a double boiler setup over low heat.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally to infuse the oil with the herbal properties.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil to the strained infusion and heat for an additional 10 minutes until melted.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with plantar fasciitis because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that target the affected area, reducing swelling and pain. Antioxidant properties: Kansas coneflower contains antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to inflammation and discomfort associated with Plantar Fasciitis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea angustifolia roots in late summer or early fall for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested roots by spreading them out in a single layer on paper towels for 7-10 days.
Grind 2 parts dried echinacea angustifolia root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder and mix with 1 part beeswax.
Melt 1/4 cup of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a double boiler over medium heat for 10-15 minutes.
Add the ground echinacea angustifolia mixture to the melted oils and stir until well combined and smooth, then pour into small jars.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves and dry them in shade at room temperature for 3-4 weeks.
Grind the dried leaves into fine powder using mortar and pestle, resulting in 100g of herbal extract.
Mix the powdered extract with 200ml of coconut oil, 50ml of beeswax, and 20ml of distilled water to create a smooth paste.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 65°c for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the wax melts and the mixture thickens.
Strain the cream through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding any solids, and allow it to cool before use.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried achillea millefolium leaves and stems in late summer or early fall, ensuring optimal potency.
Dry the harvested material using air circulation or shade drying methods for 7-10 days to preserve bioactive compounds.
Combine 2 tablespoons of dried achillea millefolium with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 4 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler, heating until melted and mixed well.
Remove from heat, let cool slightly, then strain through cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding solids after 10 minutes.

Combine 20g of the powdered vaccinium myrtillus with 100ml of organic coconut oil and stir until fully incorporated over 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Maidenhair fern has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling and pain associated with plantar fasciitis.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant compounds in maidenhair fern may help protect against damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of plantar fasciitis.
Pain-relieving: Maidenhair fern has been traditionally used to relieve pain and reduce inflammation, which may be beneficial for managing the discomfort associated with plantar fasciitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and pain, making them effective in alleviating symptoms of plantar fasciitis.
Barks: The barks have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can be extracted to create creams that soothe and calm affected areas.
remedy_recipe: Sterilize glass jars and utensils by submerging them in boiling water for 20 minutes.
Combine 50 grams of coconut oil with 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler at medium heat.
Add 10 grams of dried ginkgo biloba extract powder to the melted mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Continue heating the mixture for an additional 30 minutes, allowing it to thicken into a creamy consistency.
Remove the cream from heat and let it cool before transferring it to the sterilized glass jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey creams helps with plantar fasciitis because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and healing properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as allantoin and rosmarinic acid, work together to reduce swelling and pain in the heel and arch area. Additionally, comfrey's ability to promote tissue repair and regeneration helps to strengthen the plantar fascia, reducing strain and discomfort. Regular application of the cream can provide long-lasting relief from plantar fasciitis symptoms, allowing individuals to return to their normal activities with greater ease.
remedy_properties: Allantoin: Comfrey's allantoin content helps to promote skin regeneration and reduce inflammation, which can aid in reducing pain and discomfort associated with plantar fasciitis.

Alkaloids: The alkaloid compounds present in comfrey, such as rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help to reduce swelling and relieve pain in the affected area.

Silicones: Comfrey contains silicon, which is an essential mineral for connective tissue health. Silicon can help to strengthen and support the plantar fascia, reducing the likelihood of strain or injury.
remedy_parts: Roots: Comfrey roots contain high amounts of allantoin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce pain and swelling in the feet.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of comfrey contain rosmarinic acid, a powerful antioxidant that helps to soothe and calm inflammation in the plantar fascia.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried symphytum officinale herb and grind into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered symphytum officinale with 100 grams of sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant glass container at room temperature for 24 hours.
Infuse the mixture in a double boiler set over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to create a smooth and consistent texture.
Strain the infused mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another heat-resistant glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the herbal extract for use.

Mix one part of the reserved herbal extract with two parts of beeswax and five parts of coconut oil, heating the mixture in a double boiler set over low heat for 10 minutes to create a smooth and creamy texture suitable for application topically.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with plantar fasciitis because they contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that target the affected area. The cream's active compounds, such as calendic acid, reduce swelling and alleviate pain by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing effects soothe dry skin and relieve tension on the plantar fascia, promoting a healthy environment for tissue repair and reducing discomfort associated with plantar fasciitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Pot marigold cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like calendic acid and flavonoids, which help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold cream, such as vitamins C and E, help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy tissue repair.

Wound-healing and antimicrobial properties: Pot marigold's flavonoids and terpenes have been shown to promote wound healing and prevent bacterial infections, which can help accelerate recovery from plantar fasciitis-related wounds or ulcers.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Pot Marigold plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and pain associated with plantar fasciitis.
Seeds: The seeds of the Pot Marigold plant have been shown to possess analgesic properties, which can provide relief from pain caused by plantar fasciitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula arvensis flowers in the early morning and dry them using shade drying or air circulation methods.
Combine 20g of dried calendula flowers with 40ml of olive oil in a glass jar and store it at room temperature for two weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the infused oil from the solids and discard the solids.
Mix 100ml of beeswax pellets with 100ml of coconut oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler until fully incorporated and melted.
Combine the infused calendula oil with the beeswax-coconut oil mixture and stir well before pouring it into jars and allowing it to cool and harden.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for plantar fasciitis are topical preparations that combine natural herbs and ingredients to alleviate pain, inflammation, and stiffness associated with plantar fasciitis. These creams help by reducing swelling, promoting blood flow, and relaxing the affected area, providing relief from heel pain and discomfort. Examples of herbal creams that show promise include those containing arnica, capsaicin, and peppermint oil. By using these creams, individuals with plantar fasciitis can improve their daily activities, such as walking or running, without experiencing excruciating heel pain, allowing them to live more comfortably and confidently.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat plantar fasciitis with herbal creams is to apply topical creams containing arnica, capsaicin, or peppermint oil directly to the affected area. Arnica cream reduces inflammation and pain, while capsaicin cream increases blood flow and relieves discomfort. Peppermint oil's cooling properties help soothe and relax the foot. Use these creams regularly, ideally after warming up with a gentle massage, for optimal relief from plantar fasciitis symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to plantar fasciitis that are treated with herbal creams are conditions such as tendinitis, bursitis, and strain or sprain of muscles and ligaments. These topical creams often contain anti-inflammatory and analgesic herbs like arnica, turmeric, and willow bark, which help reduce pain and inflammation in the affected area.
